concept.conceptName: Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood by Electrophoresis
Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood by HPLC
Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood by calculation
Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood by IFCC protocol
Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood by JDS/JSCC protocol
Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total [Pure mass fraction] in Blood

en.name: Glycated hemoglobin (HbA1c) in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Glycated hemoglobin (HbA1c) is the fraction of haemoglobin that has been non-enzymatically glycated, expressed as a percentage of total haemoglobin. Because red blood cells circulate for ~ 120 days, HbA1c reflects the average plasma glucose over the preceding 8-12 weeks [1]. It is the reference biomarker for monitoring long-term glycaemic control in diabetes mellitus and, since 2010, an accepted criterion for diabetes diagnosis (HbA1c ≥ 6.5% / ≥ 48 mmol/mol) [2]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by HPLC, immunoassay or capillary electrophoresis on a whole blood sample (EDTA tube). Results are reported in two unit systems: **NGSP (%)**, the historical US convention, and **IFCC (mmol/mol)**, the SI / European convention. The two are related by `HbA1c [mmol/mol] = 10.93 × HbA1c [%] − 23.50` (Master Equation, [1]). **Note: assay interferences.** HbA1c is unreliable in conditions affecting red cell turnover (haemolytic anaemia, recent transfusion, chronic kidney disease, pregnancy, iron-deficiency anaemia) and in the presence of haemoglobin variants (HbS, HbC, HbE) — interferences depend on the assay method [1]. Source data on assay method (HPLC, immunoassay, IFCC protocol) is preserved by separate LOINC codes. ## Included Concepts The set lists eight LOINC "Lab Test" concepts for HbA1c in blood (no LOINC Hierarchy is available in the loaded vocabulary), grouped by assay method or representation. ### HbA1c / total haemoglobin ratio - *3004410 / 4548-4 — Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood (LOINC)*: most widely used. - *3003309 / 4549-2 — Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood by Electrophoresis (LOINC)* - *3005673 / 17856-6 — Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood by HPLC (LOINC)*: HPLC method. - *3007263 / 17855-8 — Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood by calculation (LOINC)* - *40762352 / 59261-8 — Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood by IFCC protocol (LOINC)*: IFCC-standardised, reported in mmol/mol. - *40765129 / 62388-4 — Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood by JDS/JSCC protocol (LOINC)*: Japanese protocol. - *42869630 / 71875-9 — Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total [Pure mass fraction] in Blood (LOINC)*: dimensionless ratio variant. ### Mass concentration - *3034639 / 41995-2 — Hemoglobin A1c [Mass/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: rarely used. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`. For the ratio codes, NGSP reports use **`%`** (concept 8554 — percent), IFCC reports use **`mmol/mol`** (concept 9579 — millimole per mole). Conversion: `HbA1c [mmol/mol] = 10.93 × HbA1c [%] − 23.50`. The `[Pure mass fraction]` variant is a dimensionless ratio between 0 and 1. ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The set deliberately omits: - *36032094 / 96595-4 — Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in DBS*: dried blood spot, different specimen. - **Cord blood and fetus specimens**: physiologically distinct from post-natal blood. - **HbA1c goal / target codes**: clinical observation, not a measurement. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports an HbA1c value without specifying method: `3004410 / 4548-4 — Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood`. The most common LOINC code. - **Source explicitly mentions HPLC, electrophoresis or another method**: map to the method-specific LOINC code if available. - **Source reports estimated average glucose (eAG)**: derived measurement, do **not** map to HbA1c. Out of scope. - **Do not confuse with**: fasting glucose, oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT), fructosamine (shorter-term glycation marker), glycated albumin. ## References [1] Sacks DB, Arnold M, Bakris GL, Bruns DE, Horvath AR, Lernmark Å, et al. Guidelines and recommendations for laboratory analysis in the diagnosis and management of diabetes mellitus. *Clin Chem*. 2023;69(8):808-68. Available: https://doi.org/10.1093/clinchem/hvad080 [2] American Diabetes Association. 2. Classification and diagnosis of diabetes: Standards of Medical Care in Diabetes-2023. *Diabetes Care*. 2023;46(Suppl 1):S19-40. Available: https://doi.org/10.2337/dc23-S002 [3] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 4548-4 — Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood. Available: https://loinc.org/4548-4/
